name: nathan-standards
description: Development standards for the Nathan n8n-Jira agent automation system.
capabilities: Provides n8n workflow templates with webhook patterns
Defines Python code conventions and module structures
Documents credential management and security patterns
Includes registry and contract specifications for automation
Offers YAML configuration patterns for workflow management
Prescribes spec-driven development using agent-os commands
author: Joseph OBrien
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing development standards and code pattern examples. No executable code, scripts, or runtime capabilities. All 148 static findings are false positives triggered by documentation code examples. Previous audit confirmed no risk factors.
valueStatement: Teams building n8n-Jira automation need consistent patterns and conventions. This skill provides standardized templates, workflow structures, and Python patterns to ensure maintainable and secure automation code.
limitations: Reference documentation only, no code execution
Does not include runtime n8n or Python libraries
Requires separate n8n instance for actual automation
Patterns are templates requiring adaptation to specific projects

useCases.title: Build n8n workflows
useCases.description: Create webhook-based n8n workflows following validated patterns with proper secret validation and response formats.

useCases.title: Write Python modules
useCases.description: Develop Python helper services with consistent type hints, async patterns, and dataclass structures.

useCases.title: Configure registries
useCases.description: Set up workflow registries and command contracts for distributed automation systems.

promptTemplates.title: Create n8n workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the standard n8n webhook workflow pattern from Nathan standards for creating a new Jira integration workflow.

promptTemplates.title: Python module template
promptTemplates.prompt: What is the recommended Python module template from Nathan standards for async webhook client implementations?

promptTemplates.title: Registry configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Give me the YAML registry format from Nathan standards for defining command endpoints and parameters.

promptTemplates.title: Expression escaping
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the correct JQL expression escaping patterns from Nathan standards for use in n8n expressions?

bestPractices: Always use shared secret authentication for n8n webhooks via X-N8N-SECRET header
Keep credentials in n8n only, never in Python services that call webhooks
Include metadata contracts in workflow settings to document input/output contracts
Use frozen dataclasses with slots for immutable data models in Python code
antiPatterns: Do not embed credentials in webhook URLs or request bodies
Avoid template literals in n8n expressions, use string concatenation instead
Do not skip secret validation in webhook workflows
Avoid mutable dataclasses when data models should be immutable

faq.question: Is n8n required to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides patterns and standards. You need a running n8n instance only if building actual workflows.

faq.question: Can these patterns work with other Jira tools?
faq.answer: Yes. Patterns focus on webhook contracts and Python structures that adapt to any REST API integration.

faq.question: Are the Python patterns async-compatible?
faq.answer: Yes. All Python patterns use async/await and include proper async HTTP client implementations.

faq.question: Is credential data safe with these patterns?
faq.answer: Yes. Patterns mandate n8n-only credential storage with shared secret webhook authentication.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything?
faq.answer: No. This is a reference skill with documentation. No runtime dependencies required.

faq.question: How does this compare to other workflow skills?
faq.answer: Focuses on n8n-Jira specific conventions rather than general automation. Provides strict security patterns.
